steps: Go to https://www.namecheap.com and log in to your account
Click your username in the top-right corner and select 'Profile'
Scroll down to the 'Business & Dev Tools' section
Toggle API Access to ON, accept the Terms of Service, and enter your account password
Your API key will be displayed — copy it
Whitelist your server's IP address by entering it in the 'Whitelisted IPs' field and clicking 'Add IP'

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: API Key
credential_fields.description: Your Namecheap API key, used together with your account username and whitelisted IP

notes: Every API request requires four parameters: ApiUser (your username), ApiKey, UserName (same as ApiUser), and ClientIp (your whitelisted IP)
Only IPv4 addresses can be whitelisted; requests from non-whitelisted IPs will be rejected
API access requires a Namecheap account with at least $50 in purchases or at least 20 domains
A sandbox environment is available at https://api.sandbox.namecheap.com for testing
Resetting the API key immediately invalidates the old key for all applications using it

test_endpoint.description: List domains in the account to verify credentials (replace params with your values)
